rationale: The citing paper uses Novemsky et al. (2007) in its theoretical setup for Experiment 5, citing it as evidence that subjective decision difficulty or fluency can affect outcome satisfaction. This is not just a bibliography-list mention; it directly supports a specific prediction about downstream satisfaction effects in the citing paper. Although the citation appears in a grouped pair, its functional role in motivating a tested hypothesis is clear.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Novemsky et al. (2007) as conceptual support that felt ease or difficulty in choosing influences post-choice satisfaction. It then uses that logic to justify testing whether its own manipulations of mental representation and perceived similarity change satisfaction via choice difficulty.
